steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 2 says it was "relatively rare" for railroad supporters to be secretive. Sentence 3 says LeMoyne and others "were vocal abolitionists" — the opposite of secretive. The relationship is frequency contrast: rare (secretive) vs. common (vocal). The blank must signal what happened more frequently.

steps.title: Match the transition to the logic
steps.content: A. "Fittingly," — signals that what follows is appropriate or expected given the circumstances. Fails: this doesn't address frequency at all; it just implies the vocal behavior was fitting, not that it was the more common pattern. B. "Granted," — signals a concession, acknowledging a point that might seem to undercut the argument. Fails: sentence 3 is not conceding anything — it reinforces the frequency claim in sentence 2. C. "More often," — directly signals frequency, completing the contrast: secretive was rare, vocal was more often the case. Correct. D. "For example," — signals that a specific illustrating instance follows. Fails: sentence 3 states a general frequency pattern ("they were vocal abolitionists"), not a specific single example, so "For example" would misrepresent the sentence's purpose. That matches choice C — C is correct.
